title: [2026] TRE 029
text:
IN THE JERSEY EMPLOYMENT AND DISCRIMINATION TRIBUNAL 

 BETWEEN 

 DENIS BARROW 

 CLAIMANT 

 AND 

 BLUE ISLANDS LIMITED (In
 Liquidation) 

 RESPONDENT 

 JUDGMENT IN DEFAULT 

 Reference : [ 2026]
TRE 29 

 Before:
 Advocate
Dr E Moran, Chairman 

 Decision 

 The Respondent must pay the Claimant damages in the sum of £15,647.03. 

 Reasons 

 1. 
 The Claimant worked for the
Respondent as a Captain. As a condition of employment 
he was required by the Respondent to pay a Type Rating Bond of £17,000. The
money would be repaid to him monthly over a period of years. 

 2. 
 His employment was
terminated on 17 November 2025 when the Respondent went into liquidation
leaving an outstanding debt of £15,647.03. 

 3. 
 The Respondent has not
repaid the money and has not filed a Response to the Claimant’s Claim Form. As
the claims are undefended, the Claimant is entitled to judgment in default
under Article 12 of the Employment and Discrimination Tribunal (Procedure)
(Jersey) Order 2016. 

 4. 
 I therefore order the Respondent
to pay the Claimant damages in the sum of £15,647.03. 

 Advocate Dr E Moran, Chairman Dated 3 February 2026 

 Page Last Updated: 11 Feb 2026
